_note: MERGED: 13 hand-verified masterworks (lead per beat) + PD/CC0 pool harvested from Wikimedia Commons categories (action=query, extmetadata PD-filter, deduped, capped ~beat_seconds/3). Each beat maps to categories of what it depicts. Curated by PlayfulProcess; all public domain / CC0; only audio is AI.

images.title: Pallas and Arachne (Athena and Arachne)
images.license: PD-Art (PD-old, faithful reproduction of 2D PD work; Tintoretto d. 1594)
images.note: Minerva watches Arachne at the loom from below. Tintoretto canvas (Uffizi). Strong opener for Minerva's brooding pride. NOTE: the alternate Commons file 'Tintoretto - Minerva e Aracne, 1575-85 (1579).jpg' was REJECTED (photographer CC-BY-SA claim); this PD-Art file is used instead.

images.title: Arachne (weaver at the spinning wheel, surrounded by textile tools)
images.artist: Philip Galle, after Marten van Cleve & Frans Floris
images.license: PD (faithful reproduction of 2D PD work; engravers 16th c.)
images.note: Arachne at the spinning wheel amid the tools of the textile trade, weaver and loom behind. Fits the humble-origin / craft-fame beat.

images.title: Las Hilanderas (The Fable of Arachne / The Spinners)
images.note: The famous Prado tapestry-workshop scene; spinners in the foreground, the contest tableau (with the tapestry of Europa) behind. Anchors Arachne's skill and her boast.

images.title: Minerva and Arachne (Minerve et Arachne)
images.note: Versailles canvas of Minerva confronting/instructing Arachne. Good for the disguised-goddess and warning beat.

images.title: Minerve et Arachne
images.note: Tense confrontation between Minerva and the defiant Arachne. Anchors Arachne's defiant reply / Minerva revealing herself.

images.title: Athena and Arachne (Metamorphoses plate)
images.note: Engraving from Tempesta's Metamorphoses series showing Athena and Arachne. Period-print register for the moment the goddess reveals herself and the contest is joined.

images.title: Athena Changing Arachne into a Spider (Metamorphoses plate)
images.license: PD (LACMA public-domain high-res release; Tempesta d. 1630)
images.note: Engraving showing both weavers at their looms; provides a contest tableau. (Despite the catalog title, the plate frames the weaving competition.) Anchors the contest beginning.

images.title: The Rape of Europa
images.note: Europa carried off on the bull's back across the waves - the exact first scene Arachne weaves (idx 9). Titian (Gardner Museum, Boston).

images.title: Leda and the Swan
images.note: Leda by the stream beneath the swan's wings - one of Jove's deceptions Arachne depicts in idx 9. Correggio (Gemaldegalerie, Berlin).

images.title: Danae
images.note: Jove luring Danae as a shower of gold - among the divine deceptions in Arachne's web. Bridges idx 9-10 (the catalogue of Jove/Neptune/Apollo seductions). Correggio (Galleria Borghese).

images.title: Las Hilanderas - detail (the contest tapestry)
images.note: Detail of the rear chamber where Minerva (helmeted) confronts Arachne before the woven tapestry - reads as the moment of judgment / Minerva's wrath at the finished web.

images.title: The Spider (Arachne transformed)
images.note: Allegorical figure of Arachne at the threshold of transformation - non-graphic. Used to cover the curse beat and (gently) bridge the merged wrath/transformation arc without depicting the hanging (idx 12).

images.title: Arachne (etching, the spider-woman)
images.note: Etching evoking Arachne with her spider/web nature - closes the stream on the transformation into the spider who weaves forever.
